name: Black Beans
summary: A dark, antioxidant-rich bean high in fiber and plant protein — part of the pulse family shown to modestly lower blood pressure.

benefits.mechanism: Beans are rich in potassium and fiber and low in sodium — a combination that supports healthy blood pressure. A meta-analysis of randomized trials found that eating non-oil-seed legumes (beans, lentils, chickpeas) reduced blood pressure.

citations.title: The effect of non-oil seed legume intake on blood pressure: a systematic review and meta-analysis of randomized controlled trials (Reyneke et al., Adv Nutr 2023)

benefits.mechanism: Black beans deliver a large dose of fiber for gut health and steadier blood sugar, and their dark seed coat is rich in anthocyanin antioxidants — the same purple-black pigments found in blueberries.

citations.title: Legume intakes on cardiometabolic profile and gut microbiome function: systematic review and meta-analyses of randomised controlled trials (Hossain et al., Eur J Nutr 2024)

surprisingFacts.text: Most of black beans' antioxidants live in the black skin — the cooking water turns purple-grey because those anthocyanins leach out.

cautions.effect: Gas and bloating from the fibre and oligosaccharides. Dried beans must be soaked and boiled properly — undercooked beans cause stomach upset.
